title: What are Vectors?
concept: A vector is a list of numbers representing a point in space, like a house's size and price grouped together.

exercises.prompt: Recap: turning patterns in data into decisions is called an insight. Why do we need math in machine learning?

options.text: To measure, manipulate, and compute those patterns automatically
To make code harder to read

exercises.explain: Right — math lets us represent patterns in numbers so computers can calculate them.

cards.title: Pinpointing a location
cards.body: When you drop a pin on GPS, you use two numbers: latitude and longitude. Together, they represent a single point. In ML, we group numbers the same way.

cards.title: What is a vector?
cards.body: A vector is simply an ordered list of numbers. In machine learning, it represents a single data point in space (like a house's size and its price grouped together: [2000, 350000]).

cards.title: GPS for data
cards.body: Think of a vector as a set of GPS coordinates, but for data. Instead of North and East, the axes might be Size and Price. The vector tells the computer exactly where that house sits on the map.

cards.title: Representing a shoe
cards.body: A running shoe can be a vector: [size, weight, price]. A size 9 shoe weighing 10oz and costing $120 is represented as the vector: [9, 10, 120].

cards.title: Not just random lists
cards.body: A vector isn't just a random pile of numbers. The order matters. If you swap size and price, a size 9 shoe costing $120 becomes a size 120 shoe costing $9!

cards.title: Single number vs. vector
cards.body: The distinction between scalar and vector:

right.text:
A grouped list of numbers
e.g. Shoe: [9, 10, 120]

exercises.prompt: What is a vector in machine learning?

options.text: An ordered list of numbers representing a point in space

exercises.explain: A vector is an ordered list of numbers representing coordinates in a multi-dimensional space.

exercises.prompt: True or false: the order of numbers inside a vector does not matter.
exercises.explain: False — the order is critical. Each position represents a specific feature (like size first, then price).

exercises.sentence: A vector represents a single point in space. For a car, the vector `[25, 4]` could represent ___ and doors.
exercises.explain: A vector groups numeric measurements like miles per gallon and door count.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is written as a mathematical vector?
exercises.explain: Vectors are represented as lists of numbers grouped inside brackets.

exercises.prompt: A house is 1,800 square feet, has 3 bedrooms, and sells for $250,000. How would a machine learning model represent this house as a vector?
exercises.explain: Perfect! Stacking the features in a single vector allows the model to treat the house as a single point in space. Next: how to stack multiple vectors into a grid!
